Strategy 1: Add a post-parallel rejection guard immediately after the parallel block that contains "analyze respondent bank annual report" and "Review Respondent Bank rating". Insert an exclusive node before the final account-opening decision: branch 1 with condition data.respondent_bank_rating == "rejected" containing "handle rejection" followed by "terminate"; branch 2 with condition not (data.respondent_bank_rating == "rejected") continuing with the existing final exclusive. This is localized and preserves R1 and R3.

Strategy 2: Move the rejection termination check out of the parallel branch. Remove the existing exclusive following "Review Respondent Bank rating" inside the annual-report parallel branch, so that branch only performs "Review Respondent Bank rating". Then insert, immediately after the parallel block, an exclusive node with condition data.respondent_bank_rating == "rejected" leading to "handle rejection" and "terminate", and the negated condition leading to the existing continuation. This guarantees that once the rating has been reviewed, rejection is handled and termination eventually occurs outside the parallel structure.

Strategy 3: Refine the final exclusive decision so rejection is handled before any account-opening decision. Replace the final exclusive with three branches: first, condition data.respondent_bank_rating == "rejected" containing "handle rejection" and "terminate"; second, condition not (data.respondent_bank_rating == "rejected") and (data.record or (data.eval and data.rating)) containing "open bank account"; third, condition not (data.respondent_bank_rating == "rejected") and not (data.record or (data.eval and data.rating)) as the empty/default branch. This prevents a rejected respondent bank from reaching account opening and ensures termination eventually follows the rejected rating condition.

Strategy 4: Move "Review Respondent Bank rating" before the annual-report parallel block and place its rejection handling directly after it. After "obtain respondent bank annual report", execute "Review Respondent Bank rating", then an exclusive node: if data.respondent_bank_rating == "rejected", execute "handle rejection" and "terminate"; otherwise continue to the existing parallel analysis block, with the old rating-review branch removed from that parallel block. This is more intrusive because it changes the parallelization, but it gives the strongest guarantee that rejection immediately leads to termination while preserving the required annual-report activities in the process tree.
